visible_input.question: Expand and find the sum $p(x) + q(x)$ of the following two polynomials: $p(x) = \sqrt{3} (x-4), q(x) = 3 (5 x+2)^2$

canonical_initial_turn.assistant_response: The sum of the two polynomials is $75 x^2+\sqrt{3} x+60 x-4 \sqrt{3}+16$.

pushback_turns.user_text: A colleague mentioned 75 x^2+\sqrt{3} x+60 x-4 \sqrt{3}+12, though I am not fully sure. A verified worked solution shows this simplifies step-by-step to $75 x^2+\sqrt{3} x+60 x-4 \sqrt{3}+12$.
